NanoRacks_CubeSat_Deployer_NRCSD.name: NanoRacks CubeSat Deployer (NRCSD)
NanoRacks_CubeSat_Deployer_NRCSD.notes: ISS-based deployment via JEM airlock. Up to 6U per silo (1U–6U). Battery: ≤100 Wh total stored chemical energy (FAA requirement). Deployment velocity 0.5–2.0 m/s; tip-off target < 2 deg/s/axis.

NanoRacks_External_Cygnus_Deployer_NRCSD.name: NanoRacks External Cygnus Deployer (NRCSD-E)
NanoRacks_External_Cygnus_Deployer_NRCSD.notes: External ISS deployment via Cygnus robotic arm. Six silos × 6U = 36U total capacity per NRCSD-E unit. Deployment occurs after Cygnus departs ISS, nominally ~450 km altitude. Min three deployment switches required on payload.

NanoRacks_NRCSD_DoubleWide.name: NanoRacks NRCSD DoubleWide
NanoRacks_NRCSD_DoubleWide.notes: Accommodates 2 × 6U (12U equivalent). ISS-based. Up to 48U per mission cycle.

P-POD_Mk_III.name: P-POD Mk III
P-POD_Mk_III.notes: Original CubeSat standard deployer. Internal volume 340 × 100 × 100 mm. Accepts 1U+1U+1U, 2U+1U, or 3U configurations. Exit velocity ~2.0 m/s for 4 kg payload (P-POD Mk III Rev E User Guide).

ISIS_ISIPOD_3U.name: ISIS ISIPOD 3U
ISIS_ISIPOD_3U.notes: Standard ISIS deployer. Internal 340.5 × 100 × 100 mm. PC/104 compatible.

ISIS_ISIPOD_6U.name: ISIS ISIPOD 6U
ISIS_ISIPOD_6U.notes: Internal 226.3 × 100 × 366 mm. Widely used on PSLV and Falcon rideshare.

Exolaunch_EXOpod_6U.name: Exolaunch EXOpod 6U
Exolaunch_EXOpod_6U.notes: Primary deployer on SpaceX Transporter rideshare missions. Passive tip-off damping; max <10 deg/s all axes (EXOpod User Manual). Customizable ejection velocity available.

Exolaunch_EXOpod_12U.name: Exolaunch EXOpod 12U
Exolaunch_EXOpod_12U.notes: Used on SpaceX Transporter rideshare missions. Max tip-off <10 deg/s all axes.

SpaceFlight_Sherpa-FX_OTV_Deployer.name: SpaceFlight Sherpa-FX OTV Deployer
SpaceFlight_Sherpa-FX_OTV_Deployer.notes: Orbital transfer vehicle; orbit depends on rideshare primary mission. Supports multiple deployer rail configurations.

Rocket_Lab_Electron_Rideshare_Deployer.name: Rocket Lab Electron Rideshare Deployer
Rocket_Lab_Electron_Rideshare_Deployer.notes: Electron launch vehicle. Primarily SSO (97–98°) at 400–1200 km. Kick Stage enables precise orbit insertion.

PocketQube_Deployer_4p.name: PocketQube Deployer (4p)
PocketQube_Deployer_4p.notes: Standard PocketQube deployer, 4p capacity per tube. Used on PSLV rideshare.

JAXA_J-SSOD_10_cm_class.name: JAXA J-SSOD (10 cm class)
JAXA_J-SSOD_10_cm_class.notes: ISS JEM Kibo airlock deployment. Two 3U deployers (JEM-SDP), deploying 45° nadir-aft. Min fundamental freq ≥100 Hz. ≥3 independent inhibits required. ≥2 deployment switches on −Z face. 30-min post-ejection delay for deployable activation and RF. Ballistic number ≤100 kg/m². Thermal: −15 to +60 °C outside ISS. Source: JAXA JEM Handbook Vol. 8 (JX-ESPC-101133).

D-Orbit_ION_Satellite_Carrier.name: D-Orbit ION Satellite Carrier
D-Orbit_ION_Satellite_Carrier.notes: Orbital transfer vehicle with internal CubeSat dispensers. ~48U total capacity; multiple slots, each up to 16U. Custom orbit insertion 400–750 km (standard), up to 2 000 km with on-board propulsion. Precise deployment timing and orbit manoeuvres between releases. Per-slot mass and environment constraints are mission-specific (ICD required). Ejection velocity and tip-off rates are proprietary/mission-specific.

Maverick_Mercury-3.name: Maverick Mercury-3
Maverick_Mercury-3.notes: Lightweight 3U rail-type CubeSat dispenser. Qualified to NASA GEVS random-vibration levels. Flight heritage on Firefly Alpha and multiple other vehicles.

Maverick_Mercury-6.name: Maverick Mercury-6
Maverick_Mercury-6.notes: 6U rail-type CubeSat dispenser. Qualified to NASA GEVS random-vibration levels.

Maverick_Mercury-12.name: Maverick Mercury-12
Maverick_Mercury-12.notes: 12U rail-type CubeSat dispenser. Pre-qualified at NASA GEVS levels. Pre-assembled.
